summary: Matched and linked all functions in src/melee/it/items/itseakneedleheld.c, moving the Sheik/Kirby held-needle item object from NonMatching to Matching. The PR added typed item-vars for the held needle owner, completed the item state table, implemented held-needle visibility/count updating and spawn logic, and tightened symbol/split metadata for local sdata/sdata2 constants.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so melee/it/items/itseakneedleheld.c is built as Object(Matching, ...) instead of Object(NonMatching, ...).
Implemented ItemStateTable it_803F70A8 with callbacks itSeakneedleheld_UnkMotion0_Anim, itSeakneedleheld_UnkMotion0_Phys, and itSeakneedleheld_UnkMotion0_Coll.

Implemented it_802B18B0(Item_GObj*), which reads the held needle item's owner, checks that ip->owner still matches, obtains the Sheik or Kirby charged-needle count, and hides or unhides up to six needle JObjs using JOBJ_HIDDEN.

Implemented it_802B19AC(Fighter_GObj* parent_gobj, Vec3* cur_pos, Fighter_Part part, ItemKind kind, float facing_dir), constructing a SpawnItem, spawning it with Item_80268B18, zeroing item command vars, storing the parent fighter as the held-needle owner, and attaching the item to a fighter part with Item_8026AB54.

Implemented itSeakneedleheld_UnkMotion0_Anim(Item_GObj*), including owner validity checks, Sheik/Kirby state checks via ftSk_SpecialS_80111F70 and ftKb_SpecialNSk_80105FF0, held-needle model update through it_802B18B0, and scale update from ftLib_800869D4.
Added itSeakNeedleHeld_ItemVars containing Fighter_GObj* owner at item variable offset ip+DD4 and added it to the Item xDD4_itemVar union as seakneedleheld.
Updated itseakneedleheld.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and generic HSD_GObj/u32 arguments to typed Item_GObj*, Fighter_GObj*, Fighter_Part, and ItemKind signatures.
Adjusted GALE01 split ranges for itseakneedleheld.c sdata and sdata2 to smaller local ranges.
Changed relevant symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt from global it_804D5468, it_804D5470, and it_804DCF88 to local labels @224, @225, and @186.
smart_moves: The item variable struct was kept minimal: itSeakNeedleHeld_ItemVars only records the owner pointer, matching the observed use at xDD4 rather than over-modeling unknown item state.

The implementation distinguishes normal Sheik held needles from Kirby copied Sheik needles with explicit ItemKind cases and calls the corresponding ftSk and ftKb helper functions for count/state queries.
The held-needle visibility update traverses the JObj sibling list and toggles JOBJ_HIDDEN per needle slot, which reflects the visual representation of up to six stored needles.
Spawn initialization explicitly zeroes velocity and itcmd vars, sets parent object fields, and forces prev_pos.z to 0.0f, making the constructor behavior concrete enough to match.
Using typed prototypes in the item header reduced ambiguity for future callsites and clarified that the constructor takes a Fighter_Part and ItemKind, not raw u32 values.
Converting literals/symbols to local @ labels and shrinking split ranges avoided leaving matched-file constants exposed as global symbols.
decomp_lessons: When matching small character item files, update both the source implementation and build metadata: configure.py must move the object to Matching, and splits/symbols often need follow-up scope/range corrections.
Held character items often store their owning Fighter_GObj in xDD4_itemVar; modeling that as a named item-vars struct makes later code much cleaner than repeatedly casting or using raw fields.
For copied-character behavior, do not assume the original fighter helper applies. This file uses separate Sheik and Kirby helper functions for both needle count and held-needle state checks.
Owner validation can be semantically important for item animation callbacks: this implementation checks both the stored owner pointer and ip->owner before continuing with model updates.
Item model state can be represented through HSD_JObj child/sibling traversal and JOBJ_HIDDEN flags; matching code may require reproducing that exact traversal structure.
Stack padding macros such as PAD_STACK were used in the constructor and animation function, showing that otherwise-clean C may still need explicit padding to preserve matching.
naming_conventions: Item variable structs follow the pattern it<CharacterItem>_ItemVars, here itSeakNeedleHeld_ItemVars.
The Item union member uses lowercase item-family naming, here seakneedleheld.
Generated or still-unknown functions retain address-style names such as it_802B18B0 and it_802B19AC until a better semantic name is established.
Motion callbacks use the existing file-specific mixed-case pattern itSeakneedleheld_UnkMotion0_Anim, _Phys, and _Coll.
Item kind constants distinguish base character and Kirby-copy versions: It_Kind_Seak_NeedleHeld versus It_Kind_Kirby_SeakNeedleHeld.
Local data symbols in symbols.txt may be represented as @NNN labels rather than global it_804D.... names when the matched object owns local constants.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(2 * 4) in it_802B19AC and PAD_STACK(3 * 4) in itSeakneedleheld_UnkMotion0_Anim to satisfy stack-layout-sensitive matching.
Used goto labels ret_true and ret_false in the animation callback, likely to mirror original control flow closely enough for a match.
Initialized SpawnItem fields in a specific order, including chained zero assignments for velocity and itcmd variables, which can matter for codegen.
Separated HSD_JObj child acquisition into explicit child and needle variables in it_802B18B0, matching the model hierarchy traversal needed to reach the needle JObjs.
Adjusted sdata and sdata2 symbol scope from global to local and reduced split range endpoints after matching revealed the true ownership/extent of local constants.

review_feedback.author: IsaacGemal
review_feedback.body: The author disclosed being new to C and reverse decompilation, using Claude/Codex/Cursor plus asm review and small-function matching to reach 100%, and expected style or nuance cleanup.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.body: Maintainer feedback was supportive: nothing was too out of place, they would clean it up a bit for study, and the contribution was appreciated.

review_feedback.author: agent
review_feedback.body: No inline review comments were present in the provided slice, so specific reviewer-requested code changes are not evidenced.

follow_up_queries: Compare itseakneedleheld.c against itseakneedlethrown.c for shared Sheik needle item-vars or naming opportunities.
Search for other held character item constructors that store parent_gobj in xDD4_itemVar to reuse the ownership-validation pattern.
Inspect any post-merge cleanup by ribbanya around PR 2215 to see which style changes were applied after the initial AI-assisted contribution.
Check whether it_802B18B0 and it_802B19AC later received semantic names after more Sheik needle work.
